creator: eimar.koort@tutamail.com
text:
Hi,

Now both HDD-s are gparted/ufs'ed. It's able to boot up with only one hdd disk connected. 

Current layout is like that (Booted up without usb bridge. When up, added bridge and did "gpart show"):

=>      63  62333889  mmcsd0  MBR  (30G)
        63      2016          - free -  (1.0M)
      2079    102312       1  fat32lba  [active]  (50M)
    104391  62228537       2  freebsd  (30G)
  62332928      1024          - free -  (512K)

=>       0  62228537  mmcsd0s2  BSD  (30G)
         0        57            - free -  (29K)
        57  62228480         1  freebsd-ufs  (30G)

=>        40  1953525088  da0  GPT  (932G)
          40  1953525088    1  freebsd-ufs  (932G)

=>        40  1953525088  diskid/DISK-202010000009  GPT  (932G)
          40  1953525088                         1  freebsd-ufs  (932G)

=>        40  1953525088  da1  GPT  (932G)
          40  1953525088    1  freebsd-ufs  (932G)

=>        40  1953525088  diskid/DISK-20201000000A  GPT  (932G)
          40  1953525088                         1  freebsd-ufs  (932G)


And just for clarification: disks are regular 2.5" sata hdd-s, which are connected over usb bridge to RPI.

Regards,
